text: The surgeon uses the NCL tissue sealer to perform blunt and sharp dissection at the hilum, separating the upper lobe branch of the superior pulmonary vein from its adventitia and the pulmonary artery. Once isolated, the vein is divided using a stapler.

The surgeon uses the Enseal device to separate the adventitia from the pulmonary artery. The truncus anterior is isolated and divided with the Echelon stapler. Station ten lymph nodes are dissected away from the mediastinum using the curved jaws of the instrument, facilitating precise dissection.

The surgeon isolates the bronchus and removes the station eleven lymph node to facilitate bronchus isolation. A bleed from a bronchial artery is encountered and efficiently controlled using the Enseal tissue sealer.
The surgeon removes lymphatic tissue from around the bronchus, while the recurrent branch of the pulmonary artery is divided without using additional ligar clips.

The surgeon divides the bronchus and proceeds with the dissection of the mediastinal pleura, separating the right upper lobe bronchus from the posterior chest wall. The Enseal device is used to safely and efficiently dissect the posterior mediastinal pleura.
The surgeon completes the division of the horizontal fissure, followed by the division of the oblique fissure, ensuring both are performed safely.
